headline: Student Loan Default Triggers Travel Ban for 145,000 Borrowers
context: Immigration Department confirmed 145,000 Malaysians face travel restrictions due to PTPTN loan defaults as of 2025.

cards.big: 145,000 Malaysians banned from traveling abroad due to PTPTN loan defaults
cards.sub: Travel ban affects employment opportunities requiring international travel

cards.big: PTPTN blacklist threshold is RM58,000 in accumulated debt including penalties
cards.sub: Interest charges can double original loan amount over 10-year default periods

cards.big: 72% of blacklisted borrowers earn below RM3,000 monthly according to PTPTN data
cards.sub: Punitive measures target those least able to pay rather than unwilling to pay

cards.big: Australia's HECS system deducts repayments only when income exceeds AUD54,000 threshold
cards.sub: Income-contingent model eliminates need for punitive enforcement mechanisms

cards.big: Restricting movement of low-income graduates does not recover loans—it destroys earning potential
Should PTPTN replace travel bans with income-contingent repayment for low earners
